questions.question: A music store sells $x$ guitars for every $3$ keyboards. If they sell $12$ keyboards, how many guitars are sold?
If $5$ ounces of coffee costs $\$2.50$, how much will $15$ ounces cost?
In a recipe, $2$ cups of flour are needed for every $\frac{1}{4}$ cup of sugar. If $1$ cup of sugar is used, how many cups of flour are needed?
A car travels $250$ miles in $5$ hours. How many miles will it travel in $10$ hours?
A group of friends want to split some money in the ratio $2:3:5$. If they have a total of $\$200$ to split, how much will the person with the ratio $5$ get?
A bakery sells $480$ loaves of bread per day. They pack the loaves in bags of $12$. How many bags will they need per day?
A water tank can hold $3000$ gallons of water. If $1200$ gallons of water are already in the tank, what percentage of the tank is filled?
A plane travels $600$ miles in $3$ hours. How many miles will it travel in $6$ hours?
A school has $300$ students. The ratio of boys to girls is $7:5$. How many boys and girls are in the school? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

A company has $250$ employees. The company wants to give a $10\%$ raise to all employees. If the total budget for raises is $\$15000$, will the company have enough money to give all employees the raise? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Make sure students show their work for all problems
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of using proportions to model real-world situations
Tip 3: Encourage students to check their units and make sure they are consistent
